item.title: About
item.description: I’ll start as a PhD student in Quantitative Marketing at Harvard Business School in 2020 Fall. I graduated from National Tsing Hua University with a BS in Quantitative Finance and completed my MS in Statistics from National Taiwan University. I am broadly interested in causal inference and machine learning in the field of marketing, especially topics in customer lifecycle management and policy measurement. My goal is to combine economic models and modern data science techniques to understand the heterogenity behind customer decisions and how firms should develope strategies to fulfill customers’ needs.

item.title: Curriculum Vitae
item.description: Education PhD in Business Administration (Quantitative Marketing), Harvard Business School, 2020 - MS in Statistics, National Taiwan University, 2015 - 2017 BS in Quantitative Finance, National Tsing Hua University, 2011 - 2015 Honors and Awards Summer Research Fellowship, Institute of Mathematics, Academia Sinica, 2015 Honorary Membership of The Phi Tau Phi Scholastic Honor Society, 2015 First Place, International ICT Innovative Service Awards, 2014 Sponsorship of College Student Research, Ministry of Science and Technology, 2014 Hui-Chun Chin and Tsung-Dao Lee Chinese Undergraduate Research Endowment, 2014 Dean’s Award of Outstanding Academic Performance, NTHU, 2014 Research Experience Summer Student, Institude of Mathematics, Academia Sinica, 2015 Undergraduate Researcher, College of Economics, Peking University, 2014 Teaching Experience Instructor, Data Science in Banking, Taiwan Academy of Banking and Finance, 2020 Instructor, R and Text Mining, Hahow E-learninng Platform, 2019 Instructor, R and Business Analytics, Hahow E-learninng Platform, 2018 Teaching Assistant, Statistics, National Taiwan University, 2015 Industry Experience Data Lead, Migo Entertainment, 2019 - 2020 Consultant, Mastercard Data & Services (formerly Applied Predictive Technologies), 2018 - 2019 Data Scientist, InrayTek, 2017 - 2018 Intern Data Scientist, Yoctol, 2016 Intern Data Scientist, Line TV, 2016

item.title: Teaching
item.description: R and Text Mining (in Chinese) This is an introductory text mining course for students with basic knowledge in R programming. In this course, we introduced a variety of basic principles, techniques and modern advances in text mining. Paid Student: 322 (04/05/2020) Review: 4.75 / 5.00 (04/05/2020) .my-table-2 table { width: 100%; line-height:5px} Topics Case Study Introduction Basic R Programming Word Segmentation, and Regular Expression Text Mining on Tang Poetry Exploration Keyword Extraction and Visualization Content Analytics on Youtube Travel Videos Vector Space Model and Word Embedding Customer Feedback Loop for E-commerce Platform Topic Modeling: MF, PCA and LDA Topic Identification for Political News Document Representation and Classification Movie Review and Sentiment Classification R and Business Analytics (in Chinese) This is an introductory analytics course for everyone interested in data sicence and analytics.
